source.title: ALEXA Log C Curve - Usage in VFX
source.author: Harald Brendel

notes: Encode: y = (x > cut) ? c*log10(a*x + b) + d : e*x + f. Decode: x = (t > e*cut + f) ? (10^((t - d)/c) - b)/a : (t - f)/e. EI800 parameters from the appendix table (normalized sensor signal to Log C): cut = 0.010591, a = 5.555556, b = 0.052272, c = 0.247190, d = 0.385537, e = 5.367655, f = 0.092809. The document (page with 'signal corresponding to 18% gray') states 18% grey maps to Log C 0.391 = 400/1023. The paper recommends using EI800 parameters for all EIs in VFX exchange. Cross-checked 2026-07-19 against colour-science 0.4.7 log_decoding_ARRILogC3: decode(400/1023) = 0.18000002, exact.

vectors.name: grey18
vectors.quote: signal corresponding to 18% gray to the Log C value of 0.391, which is 400/1023

vectors.name: signal_f_at_zero
vectors.quote: EI800 linear-branch intercept f = 0.092809 (x = 0 encodes to y = f)
